description: Normal-road left-crossing truck enters from the ego’s front-left on a deterministic collision course while the usual right-side escape corridor is occupied by a pedestrian/blocked curb and a follower is close behind the ego. Sweep the short actionable TTC band below the existing right-blocked/pedestrian rules to expose when ego should still brake, attempt a constrained left avoidance/T-drift away from the pedestrian, or accept a front impact rather than endanger the escape-side pedestrian.
hypothesis: With the right escape side blocked by a pedestrian, the dominant maneuver should switch from right T-drift at moderate TTC/high crossing speed to braking or constrained left avoidance as TTC decreases and ego/follower closing speed rises; the boundary should occur around TTC 1.0–1.3 s and depend strongly on lateral crossing speed.
